The atmospheres of these objects were the first location where it was hypothesized that stabilized excited electrons could cause chemical bonds to rearrange orthogonal to the field. In binary systems, accretion of mass onto these objects can create supernovae with characteristic light curves, which can be used as a standard candle. For 10 points, name these stellar remnants whose electron degeneracy pressure prevents their collapse when their mass is under the Chandrasekhar limit.
